key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings denser and more wear-resistant, though with a slightly higher friction coefficient.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing barrel filling ratio is a practical process lever for improving coating integrity and tribological durability in severe wear and high-temperature components.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratios under identical conditions.
Compared microstructure and phase composition using microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Evaluated dry sliding wear with ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% coating showed reduced porosity and achieved a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N) under the tested conditions.
watch_item: The improved wear resistance at 53% came with higher friction, so application tradeoffs should be checked under the target load and speed regime.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
